fields.description:
# WS1 — Diff context-assembler + base reviewer

## Why
The four-pass code-review gate (parent b744) consumes the shipped review kernel, which needs (a) a `context` string the Pass-2 verifier re-grounds against — for code review that is the DIFF, not plan text — and (b) a Pass-1 base reviewer that always runs and emits the bounded overlay-escalation signal. This story builds those two foundations everything else wires onto.

## What
- A diff context-assembler that turns a `(base, head)` range / unified diff + changed-files list into the kernel `context` string (parallel to plan-review's `assemble_context(...).plan_text`).
- The base code-reviewer finder prompt (front-matter contract) that emits Pass-1 evidence findings AND `recommend_overlays: [{overlay_id, reason}]`, with `overlay_id` constrained to the FIXED overlay-catalog enum (the model cannot invent an overlay; a concrete one-line reason is required).
- The `recommend_overlays` output contract (a registered output schema) so the workflow can read it as a named step output.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` — the diff context assembler. It RE-IMPLEMENTS the minimal git diff-read (the small `_git` / `_changed_from_diff` / orientation / compose helpers) so it is SELF-CONTAINED and does NOT import from `code_review.py` (the to-be-retired single-pass demo). "Reuses the existing diff read" means it follows the same logic/shape, copied — not an import dependency. This keeps WS4's retirement of `code_review.py` from breaking `assemble.py`.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` — the OWNED closed `OVERLAY_IDS` enum constant (see starter set below). WS2 adds catalog CONTENT keyed by this enum.
- `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md` — base reviewer prompt (front-matter `inputs`/`outputs`/`execution_mode`/`dimension`).
- The base reviewer output schema (in `src/rebar/schemas/` + the prompt `outputs:` binding) — a SINGLE structured schema carrying both `findings` and `recommend_overlays` (see Output-wiring below).
- OUT OF SCOPE: the overlay catalog CONTENT itself (WS2), the union/dispatch (WS3).

## Overlay-id ENUM — starter set (WS1 OWNS this; resolves the WS2 cross-dependency + the E2/T8 blocking finding)
WS1 defines the closed `OVERLAY_IDS` enum as a small shared constant (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py`). WS2 authors the catalog CONTENT (overlay finder prompts + `applies_to` globs) keyed by these ids — it adds content, never new ids. The starter set is the 11 specialist overlays drafted on the parent epic:

```
OVERLAY_IDS = (
    "security",        # authn/authz, secrets, injection, unsafe deserialization
    "performance",     # hot paths, N+1, allocation, complexity regressions
    "i18n",            # localization / encoding / locale-sensitive formatting
    "a11y",            # accessibility (UI/markup/ARIA)
    "db-migrations",   # schema/data migrations, backfills, expand-contract
    "docs",            # user/operator/API docs that must track the change
    "supply-chain",    # dependency / lockfile / vendoring / provenance changes
    "api-compat",      # public API / wire / CLI / config backward-compatibility
    "iac",             # infrastructure-as-code (Terraform/CDK/K8s/Helm/Ansible)
    "tests",           # test sufficiency / regression coverage for the change
    "llm-prompts",     # prompt/contract/output-schema changes to LLM surfaces
)
```

This is the closed vocabulary the base reviewer's `recommend_overlays.overlay_id` is validated against. AC3's drop-test asserts a valid id (e.g. `"security"`) survives and an invalid id (e.g. `"made-up"`) is dropped. The list lives in code (configurable/extensible by adding an id here + WS2 content); the schema enum is generated from it so the two cannot drift.

## Output-wiring — how ONE agent step exposes two named outputs (resolves T1/T3/T8 advisories)
The v3 engine flattens an agent step's structured output dict into `steps.<id>.outputs`, so the TWO named outputs are simply two TOP-LEVEL KEYS of one structured output schema:
- The base step runs with `mode=structured` and `output_schema=code_review_base_output` (a single registered schema) — NOT `mode='findings'` (that path runs `finalize_findings()`, which would discard everything except `findings`, dropping `recommend_overlays`). This is the load-bearing wiring detail.
- `code_review_base_output.schema.json` (registered in `src/rebar/schemas/`) shape: `{ findings: [common.finding], recommend_overlays: [{overlay_id: <OVERLAY_IDS enum>, reason: string REQUIRED maxLength 200}] }`. `findings` required; `recommend_overlays` optional (defaults to empty).
- Downstream the workflow reads `steps.base.outputs.findings` (flows into merge → Pass-2) and `steps.base.outputs.recommend_overlays` (flows into WS3's `overlay_union`). The batch runner aggregates only `findings`; the base reviewer is therefore a STANDALONE agent step (not a batch criterion) so its `recommend_overlays` survives.

## Context-string shape (resolves the E2/F1 advisory)
`assemble.py` produces the kernel `context` string as a structured-markdown composite (the same shape `code_review.py._compose_context` produces, re-implemented here): a `## Changed files (N)` list, a `## Orientation` directory grouping, and a `## Diff` fenced block with the unified diff (capped at a configured char budget with an explicit truncation notice). This string is what `verify_findings(..., context=...)` re-grounds Pass-2 against. The assembler exposes it via a `DiffContext` dataclass (parallel to `PlanContext`) with a property returning this string.

## Coverage-gap note shape (resolves the E1 advisory)
The "coverage-gap note" emitted on base-reviewer failure is a finding-shaped record `{dimension: "coverage-gap", severity: "low", detail: "<why>", reviewer_id: "code-review-base"}` carried in the base step's findings list (so it flows through merge → verify → the verdict's `coverage`/`coaching`, where WS3/WS4 surface it). It is advisory, never a synthetic BLOCK (the base reviewer is recall-side).

## Specifics pinned (plan-review remediation 2026-06)
- **Overlay-id ENUM ownership:** WS1 OWNS the closed enum (above); WS2 authors content keyed by it. AC3's enum validation is fully satisfied within WS1.
- **"CI drift gate" = the existing derived-prompt-index drift check.** Observable in-session: `rebar workflow validate` passes; `python -c "from rebar.llm.prompts import get_prompt; get_prompt('code-review-base')"` resolves; `python -m rebar.llm.prompts regenerate-index` yields no diff.
- **Stakeholder:** the rebar maintainers operating the Gerrit code-gate (and downstream contributors whose changes the gate reviews).
- **Thresholds/limits are config, not magic constants:** the `reason ≤200 chars` bound and the diff char-cap are schema/config values, adjustable without code-shape changes (noted for the T5e advisory).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A diff context-assembler produces the kernel `context` string (the structured-markdown composite above) from a `(base, head)` range or a unified-diff input + changed-files list; unit-tested on a sample diff.
- [ ] `OVERLAY_IDS` is defined as a closed enum constant in `code_review/registry.py` (the 11-value starter set above); the `recommend_overlays` schema enum is derived from it so they cannot drift.
- [ ] The base reviewer prompt is a contract-bearing front-matter prompt that resolves via the prompt library and passes the CI drift gate.
- [ ] The base reviewer's output carries `recommend_overlays`, each `overlay_id` validated against `OVERLAY_IDS`; out-of-enum ids are dropped (not errored), with a test asserting the drop.
- [ ] The base reviewer runs as a STANDALONE agent step with `mode=structured` + `output_schema=code_review_base_output`, so BOTH `findings` and `recommend_overlays` survive as named step outputs (a test reads both); it is NOT a batch criterion and NOT `mode='findings'`.
- [ ] Base-step failure fallback: error/timeout/empty structured output → empty base findings + NO base-driven escalations (Round-B membership = glob-triggered overlays only) + a `coverage-gap` finding note; never auto-BLOCK (the base reviewer is recall-side; deterministic Pass-2/3 still runs on the findings that exist). Offline test asserts it.
- [ ] `assemble.py` has no import from `code_review.py`; a test pins the import path survives the WS4 retirement.

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- recommend_overlays output contract PINNED: `{overlay_id: <OVERLAY_IDS enum>, reason: str (REQUIRED, ≤200 chars)}`, carried as a top-level key of the single `code_review_base_output` schema alongside `findings` (see Output-wiring).
- Base-reviewer FAILURE fallback: empty base findings + glob-only Round-B + coverage-gap note; never fail-closed to BLOCK.
- Diff-assembler contract vs WS4: `assemble.py` is a NEW self-contained module under `src/rebar/llm/code_review/` and does NOT import from the to-be-retired `code_review.py`.
